subtitle: Sunday Dress, Stiff & Biting
location_text: St. Jude's Acadian Church, parking lot, Sunday morning

ciphers.encoding_hint: Maya's recurring body-felt sentence — the dress that physically bites is the inherited form (sacrament, doctrine, Sunday-best conformity). The dress as the actual hierophant.

ciphers.text: crossed keys above the rose window
ciphers.encoding_hint: Petrine keys: heaven and earth. In Maya's POV they sit unreachable above her, a system she's being inducted into without consent.

ciphers.text: triple papal tiara on the hierophant figure
ciphers.encoding_hint: three tiers = teaching / governing / sanctifying. Three rings the child must pass through.

ciphers.text: two kneeling acolytes — M and Y — Maya beside an unnamed companion
ciphers.encoding_hint: M = Maya. Y = ? (possibly her companion in the church scene — check vol5_ch5 prose). The pair recurs in catechism scenes; finding Y's identity unlocks a side path.

ciphers.text: 8-segment rose window — red / blue / yellow / green alternating
ciphers.encoding_hint: 8 segments and 4 colors — matches the sacraments-of-seven plus baptism doubled, or the 8 beatitudes. Counts as a 'collect the colors' puzzle if extended.
carpet stripes carry liturgical texts. The MASS schedule grounds the chapter at 'before the 7am mass' — dawn / hot Acadian summer.
'Hear and be silent.' The chapter's command to Maya. The chapter's command to the player.
ciphers.priority: HIGH — chapter thesis in two words

ciphers.text: the bishop's crozier — identical curl, identical bands — is Dante's ceremonial staff from the throne room
ciphers.encoding_hint: DORMANT until you've heard the Emperor's theme (clicking Dante's ankh on card IV). The classical Emperor(IV) → Hierophant(V) adjacency is civil authority → religious authority — and Modern Mythology renders it as the SAME held object in two vestments. AVTORITAS POTESTAS IMPERIVM (the throne room) and AVDI ET TACE (the church) are the two halves of how the institution speaks: imperium gives the command, sacramentum requires the silence that hears it.

ciphers.text: a dark circular lens reflected in the blue segment of the rose window — Elicia's camera, three rows back
ciphers.encoding_hint: DORMANT until you've touched Elicia's camera on the Priestess card. Priestess(II) is the canonical OBSERVER of every sacrament; she sits in the pew, she records, she does not partake. The Hierophant performs; the Priestess archives the performance. The same II/V opposition the deck uses everywhere — celebrant ↔ witness.

hotspots.notes: DORMANT: appears only after the player has clicked Dante's ankh on the Emperor card. The bishop's crozier in the church's left hand is — line for line — Dante's ceremonial staff in different vestments. Classical IV↔V adjacency: civil order and religious order are wielded by the same hand. AVTORITAS POTESTAS IMPERIVM ↔ AVDI ET TACE — both halves of the authority compact.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has touched Elicia's camera on the Priestess card. The same lens — circular, dark, unmistakable — is now visible reflected in one of the rose window's blue segments. The archivist is in the church before the rite, behind the player, documenting what AUDI ET TACE forbids speaking of. Priestess II is the canonical observer of every sacrament she herself declines to perform.

ENTRY HOTSPOT: clicking the left side of the painted card dispatches daemon_observer.05 to fetch the three booth calls Father Quent placed between 06:35 and 06:42 CT this Sunday morning. The vestibule booth is three rows back from the rose window, behind the painted frame; the diorama shows three handsets and a transcript-scroll on pick-up. Call 1 (Antonio) is the V → VII chain origin; Call 2 (no answer) is the V → XII zone-shifted decline Natalie logs at 23:47 PT; Call 3 (unidentified, 'don't take Carlie') is the deck's deepest vol5 withhold.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has read Erica's marginalia on the 13 Feb Quent triple-call row in the Wheel diorama. The AVDI ET TACE banner now visibly carries a faint extra word in fine ink beneath the Latin: 'CAVTE' — 'with caution.' Father Quent's rule for himself is 'hear and be silent — with caution.' The deck's reading of the chapter's thesis was always partial; the rest of the rule is for the priest who made Alberto say 'him' twice on the line because he WANTED to be heard saying it. The painted chapter shows the institution speaking; the diorama surfaced the institution recording itself.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has recognized the Hierophant's crozier in the Tower's fault line. The bishop's staff in the painted card now shows a hairline crack at the same point the Tower's wall fractures — the institutional spine carries its own future snap. V → XVI mirror reads both directions: the Tower shows where the snap will be; the Hierophant shows the spine waiting to be snapped. The crack is in the wood before lightning finds it.

easter_eggs: the chapter's full title is the actual command on Maya — 'Sunday Dress, Stiff & Biting' as both literal physical sensation and metaphor for inherited form
the second acolyte 'Y' is unnamed in the visual but referenced — possibly Father Quent's nephew or Father Paul's altar boy; check ch5 prose for the named companion
the carpet's three Latin lines are layered: REFORMANDA (always reforming) / SANCTIFICA ME (sanctify me) / MASS SCHEDULE (the actual time grid). Three registers — historical, personal, institutional.

cross_references.child_pov_paired_with: high_priestess.json (Elicia is the adult-observer to Maya's child-experiencer)
cross_references.two_kneeling_pair_with: fool.json::dog_companion (the Fool also has a single companion figure)
